title: Netherlands Sets out Draft Targets for Green Hydrogen-Based Fuels in Transport by 2030
content:
The Dutch Ministry for Infrastructure and Water Management has published a new draft of its proposed transposition of transport targets in the EUâ€™s updated Renewable Energy Directive (RED III), which require 1% of fuels to be green hydrogen or its derivatives by 2030.

The Netherlands will replace its existing HBE renewable fuel credits, based on the uptake of biofuels, with a broader system for emissions-reduction credits (known as EREs) in compliance with RED III, from 1 April 2026.
author: Analysts Account
